entry.title: Manage Your SAP Business AI Features from a Single Place: Increased Flexibility for Cloud Customers
entry.content:
By enabling autonomous activation and deactivation of SAP Business AI features, this new offering will not only empower you to adapt to changing business needs but also help you optimize operations and reduce costs. This is particularly valuable in today's fast-paced business environment, where agility and cost-efficiency are crucial for success.

Going forward, these AI features will also be sorted into a business context of processes and capabilities, reflecting your enterprise structure, business demands, and landscape design. This will allow you to scope the right set of solutions and a fitting set of application tenants.

Looking ahead, the activation of SAP Business AI features will be integrated into SAP Central Business Configuration as the central tool to manage your scope and configuration. This is the first step of bringing our vision for SAP Central Business Configuration to life: a 360° business-oriented scoping experience, enabling you to:

  • Select business processes and capabilities based on your needs
  • Simply replicate existing processes to new locations and legal entities
  • Clearly trace your activities from business request to implementation
  • Get insights and recommendations to optimize your company’s footprint and processes

We have put this vision into a short video, showing how a kids toy company brings its business to life in no time with SAP Central Business Configuration – the future of business transformation.

SAP Central Business Configuration: Vision Demo 

Please note that this is a demo video. The functionalities shown are not yet available.

All of this underscores SAP's dedication to continuously improving and streamlining user experience, making it easier for businesses to leverage AI features to drive growth and success.

Stay tuned for more!

entry.title: RAP based Fiori application implemented with Custom Entities - License Compliance Digital AccessV2.0
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

In this post, I wanted to share my knowledge and experience about “A RAP based Fiori application implemented with Custom Entities called License Compliance Digital Access V2.0 which would be available from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408”.

"Aim of this blog post is to demonstrate the abilities of custom entities in RAP and create a Fiori application with composition relationships highlighting multiple challenges and learnings".

 

It’s a standard guideline for developing Fiori apps with SAP S/4HANA recommended practices like RAP as the backend implementation, OData as the Service provider and Fiori as UX

In RAP there are multiple approaches available to build a Fiori app and it is recommended to consider the below approaches using RAP entities.

New/Greenfield Implementation

Implementation to be built from scratch

RAP BO with Managed Implementation

Brownfield Implementation

Implementation to be reused from legacy

RAP BO with Unmanaged Implementation

The above approaches are good for below cases.

  • Transactional application with CRUD operations
  • Data model with Parent child relationship between the entities
  • With features which corresponds to Behavior definition & implementation
  • Predefined data sources for defining the RAP entities.

But we cannot rely on RAP view entities when we wanted an app with the below scenarios:

  • Data model with unrelated entities
  • Complete custom business logic for each dataset
  • Data is mostly derived out of application layer logic since there is no predefined data source available.
  • Display only app where Behavior definition is not required.

In these scenarios, RAP Custom entities would be beneficial.

 

Use case:

We have a Fiori app which is already available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402 release called License Compliance Digital Access and we had a motivation to create a successor app with new functionalities.

Our motivation to create a new successor app

  • Requires incompatible changes to existing UI.
  • The existing app doesn’t support custom date range for selection of license entitlements.
  • Performance degradation over the period due to creation of large volume of digital access documents which couldn’t be improved.

So, we have come across a point where we must build our app with the latest recommended guidelines as per SAP S/4HANA in terms of UX design, RAP modelling and OData V4.

So below is the way the app should be transformed.

Key Attributes

Existing App

Successor App

UI Template

Free Style

Fiori Element

UI Technology

SAP UI5

SAP Fiori

OData Version

OData V2

OData V4

Backend Implementation

SEGW Classes

RAP Custom Entities

 

Overview of License Compliance Digital Access V2.0

Displays the consumption of Digital Access Licenses by Customer.

Key Features

  • View details about the total license entitlement, Usage of the acquired licenses for a given period.
  • View details about individual licenses
  • View details about the count, weighted count, and total weighted count of document items for each document type
  • View active and deleted instances of metered data for a document created on a particular date.


Access License Compliance Digital Access V2.0 in Fiori Apps Library

*Note: The blog is relevant for SAP S/4HANA OP 2022 or higher, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, SAP BTP, ABAP Environment and currently for the old app since new app would be available from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. Check out System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for additional details System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud


Now we will understand the technical aspects of the app along with the challenges / learnings.

Unmanaged Query - Custom Entities

Architecture

 

*Diagram illustrating the design of custom entities

 

 

*Diagram illustrating RAP BO custom entities hierarchy

 

  • The data model for an unmanaged query can be defined in a CDS custom entity. A custom entity defines the structure of the data returned by the query. This is done using CDS syntax in a CDS data definition (DDLS).
  • The data source for a CDS custom entity does not have an SQL view to select data from a database. Instead, the custom entity specifies an ABAP class that implements the query.

 

 

@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_OVERVIEW'​

 

 

          The above annotation is used to reference the query implementation class in the data definition of the CDS custom entity.

  • The ABAP annotation is evaluated when the unmanaged query is executed whereby the query implementation class is called to perform the query. For every query request on the CDS custom entity, the implementation class is instantiated. The class instance is never cached.
  • In a Business Object a custom entity can be an entity, for example a root, a parent, or a child entity using root and composition relationships. Custom entities may also be used as targets in the definition of associations and define associations as a source.
  • Access control needs to be implemented manually in the query implementation class to ensure that only those records are returned for which the user is allowed to access. You cannot use an access control object (DCL) for a custom entity.
  • Interface IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER - Unmanaged queries are implemented in ABAP classes. The query implementation class implements a predefined ABAP interface (IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER) to ensure that the required basic OData support is enabled.
    • Method select - When implementing the query, you must know that the implementation class only offers one method which is called “select”. The method select must be implemented in custom entity scenarios. It replaces the SQL-SELECT of a CDS view to retrieve and return data
    • The select imports an interface instance for the request data and one for the response data:
      • Interface IF_RAP_QUERY_REQUEST
      • Interface IF_RAP_QUERY_RESPONSE

 

Important points to consider while implementing a custom entity.

  • Initially, the code checks if the data is being requested or not to continue for further execution which is checked with below method call

 

 

io_request->is_data_requested( )

 

 

  • It is important to note that you must return the number of entries found, even though if a single request is returned otherwise no data will be shown in the object page. This is done using the below statement

 

 

io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( lt_items ) ).

 

 

  • This number should not exceed the number of entries that have been requested by the client or, the number that has been enforced by the framework if no $top and $skip has been used.
  • The data both for the single read as well as for queries is returned as an internal table to the framework.

 

 

io_request->set_data( lt_items ).

 

 

 

 

Challenges/Learnings:

We had many challenges during the development of this app which we would like to highlight here.

1. Multiple views on a List Report with different entity sets.

We had a requirement to cater the design of old free style UI into the predefined Fiori template offered by SAP.

In this process we need to have two views in the Fiori list page which is not the default solution offered by Fiori standard template, we now have to go through a minor UI breakout in order to achieve this, we have also used concept of SelectionVariants, PresentationVariants, and SelectionPresentationVariants

 Please refer to the screenshot displaying two views on list report

 

Below are the steps to achieve this:

  1. First create an entity for the second view which is used as the data source, Use below code snippet in Main entity

 

 

.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
  id: 'id_Header',
  text: 'Header',
  selectionVariantQualifier: 'SVHeader',
  presentationVariantQualifier: 'PVHeader',
  qualifier: 'Header'
},
{
  id: 'id_Items',
  text: 'Items',
  selectionVariantQualifier: 'SVItems',
  presentationVariantQualifier: 'PVItems',
  qualifier: 'Items'
}]
@UI.selectionVariant: [
  {
    text: 'Header',
    qualifier: 'SVHeader'
  },
   {
    text: 'Items',
    qualifier: 'SVItems'
  }
]
@UI.presentationVariant: [
  {
    qualifier: 'PVHeader',
   visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM }]
  },
    {
    qualifier: 'PVItems',
   visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM }]
  }
]

 

 

     2. As a next step, Insert the below code snippet in the second entity which is to be displayed as another view

 

 

.selectionPresentationVariant: [
{
  id: 'id_Items',
  text: 'Items',
  selectionVariantQualifier: 'SVItems',
  presentationVariantQualifier: 'PVItems',
  qualifier: 'Items'
}]
@UI.selectionVariant: [
   {
    text: 'Items,
    qualifier: 'SVItems'
  }
]

@UI.presentationVariant: [
    {
    qualifier: 'PVItems',
   visualizations: [{type: #AS_LINEITEM }]
  }
]

 

 

        3. Now we navigate to UI project in BAS (Business Application Studio/Web IDE) and Insert/Modify the below code in the location Webapp/Manifest.json file in the targets section.

 

 

"targets": {
        "ItemsOverviewList": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "ItemsOverviewList",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ListReport",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "contextPath": "/ItemsOverview",
              "views": {
                "paths": [
                  {
                    "key": "id_Items",
                    "entitySet": "ItemDetail",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#Items"
                  },
                  {
                    "key": "id_Header",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#Header"
                  }
                ]
              }
​

 

 

2. Dynamic facet handling

This was a huge challenge where we had to call a page by navigation dynamically i.e., We must call Page3 from the navigation of Page 2 dynamically based on the document type selected on page 2. And we have almost 19 document types

We wanted to achieve this functionality without creating UI breakout and do with cds entities only.

Hence, we have come up with an idea to handle the facet dynamically during navigation from page2 to page 3, For this we followed the below steps:

  1. We have created Fields in Page 2 custom entity where we identify each field to each facet of the document type i.e., field IsSalesDocumentHidden represents facet of Sales Document, we then used this field to control the facet Dynamically as shown below.

 

 

// Fields to control Dynamic Facet Handling for 3rd Page
@EndUserText.label             : 'Sales Document Hidden'
@UI.hidden                     : true
IsSalesDocumentHidden;

 

 

     2. We have created Parent child composition for all the child’s for each document type of the entity like below

 

 

@ObjectModel.filter.enabled    : false
_SalesDocumentList             : composition [0..*] of C_SalesDocumentList;

 

 

     3. We have created facet for every document type which corresponds to its entity like the below code snippet

 

 

  // Dynamic facet handling based on Document type selection
  .facet                      : [

  // Sales Document
  {
      id                         : '_SalesDocumentList',
      purpose                    : #STANDARD,
      position                   : 20,
      label                      : 'Items',
      type                       : #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
      hidden                     : #(IsSalesDocumentHidden),
      targetElement              : '_SalesDocumentList'
  }

 

 

     4. Now we navigate to the UI project in BAS, and we have defined the routing configuration in Manifest.json

           a. Routes (Please refer to attached file - Define Routes)

           b. Targets ((Please refer to attached file - Define Targets) 

     5. Now we have to implement the logic to handle the facet in the custom entity by populating the field corresponding to the document type (Please refer to attached file - Handle display facet in UI using flag indicator).

 

3. Handling of data between List and Object page by segregation of code in custom entity

 For each custom entity there is only one implementation class possible.

Now this custom entity has both List page and Object page.

So, we use filters to differentiate between list and object page.

When the navigation happens from the List to object page then we will set the keys as part of filters, we will assume it to be the object page and query the database with the keys or delete all the other entries expect the one matching the key, please find the code snipped for the same below

 

 

" Delete all other data in case of object page header.
IF lv_DocumentItemID IS NOT INITIAL.
 DELETE lt_Itemsoverview_data WHERE DocumentItemID NE lv_DocumentItemID.
ENDIF.

 

 

4. Adapting free style UI to one of the Fiori standard templates – DATA POINT Annotations

In the existing old app, we have data displayed in the header like the KPIs but whereas in the Fiori list object page it is not possible to show data in the header, so we made use of DATA POINT annotations to show the data as to meet the UX requirements in the old app, (please find code snippets of the same attached - Datapoint annotations)

5. We are the first team to implement metadata extension to the custom entities since it was only available from the current release.

 

 

Related Blogs:

You can find some of the related blogs for your reference below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-implement-a-custom-entity-in-the-abap-restful-programming-model/ba-p/13406280

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-abap-rap-custom-entities-with-compositions-relationship-in-a-fiori/ba-p/13577865

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-a-fiori-elements-app-for-a-rap-bo-with-transport-selection/ba-p/13562792

https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap-opensap/blob/main/week5/unit3.md

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-a4c-create-custom-entity..html

https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/b6b59e4a4c3548cf83ff9c3b955d3ba3

 

 

Conclusion:

In this blog post, we learned about end-to-end implementation of RAP based Fiori application with unmanaged query i.e., Custom entities

I'd want to express my gratitude to everyone who offered assistance to me in writing this content especially my development colleagues @Mukund_Anand_Sinha and @sachin_b 

Hope you had a great time reading through the blog. Please Like, Follow and Share if you are interested in learning more on this topic, Also Kindly share your feedback or thoughts in the comments section below
 

Sending Good Vibes!
Shiva

entry.title: SIOS LifeKeeper for High Availability of SAP ASCS /ERS HA and HANA Database on Azure
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP HANA is a high-performance in-memory database that supports real-time analytics and transactions. It is widely used by enterprises to run their critical business applications and processes. However, SAP HANA systems also require high availability and disaster recovery solutions to ensure business continuity and data protection in case of failures or disasters.

Azure is a cloud platform that offers a range of services and features to host, manage, and scale SAP HANA systems. Azure provides high-performance virtual machines, storage, networking, and security options that can meet the requirements of SAP HANA workloads. However, Azure does not offer a native high availability solution for SAP HANA systems, which means that customers need to use third-party tools or custom scripts to achieve high availability for their SAP HANA databases on Azure.

SIOS LifeKeeper is a proven high availability and disaster recovery solution that can protect SAP HANA systems on Azure. SIOS LifeKeeper can monitor, detect, and recover from failures of SAP HANA instances, virtual machines, storage, network, or Azure services. SIOS LifeKeeper can also replicate data between primary and secondary SAP HANA systems, and perform automatic or manual failover and failback operations. SIOS LifeKeeper can integrate with Azure services such as Azure Load Balancer, Azure Traffic Manager, and Azure Site Recovery to provide a comprehensive and reliable high availability solution for SAP HANA systems on Azure.

Prerequisites

Before you can configure and deploy a highly available SAP HANA system on Azure using SIOS LifeKeeper, you need to have the following prerequisites:

  • An Azure subscription and an Azure account with sufficient permissions to create and manage resources.
  • A SAP HANA license and installation media.
  • A SIOS LifeKeeper license and installation media.
  • Two or more Azure virtual machines that meet the SAP HANA hardware and software requirements. You can use the Azure Marketplace to create SAP HANA certified virtual machines.
  • A shared storage solution that supports SAP HANA data and log volumes. You can use Azure NetApp Files, Azure Files, or Azure Shared Disks as shared storage options.
  • A network solution that allows communication between the SAP HANA systems and the SIOS LifeKeeper nodes. You can use Azure Virtual Network, Azure VPN Gateway, or Azure ExpressRoute as network options.
  • A load balancer solution that can distribute traffic to the SAP HANA systems and the SIOS LifeKeeper nodes. You can use Azure Load Balancer, Azure Application Gateway, or Azure Traffic Manager as load balancer options.

Configuration Steps

After you have prepared the prerequisites, you can follow these steps to configure and deploy a highly available SAP HANA system on Azure using SIOS LifeKeeper:

  1. Install SAP HANA on the primary virtual machine and configure the SAP HANA data and log volumes on the shared storage.
  2. Install SAP ASCS  on the primary virtual machine and SAP ERS on Secondary Virtual Machine and /sapmnt on the shared storage.
  3. Install SIOS LifeKeeper on the primary virtual machine and join it to a SIOS LifeKeeper cluster.
  4. Create a SIOS LifeKeeper SAP HANA resource hierarchy on the primary virtual machine and configure the SAP HANA instance details and the shared storage paths.
  5. Install SAP HANA on the secondary virtual machine and configure the SAP HANA data and log volumes on the shared storage.
  6. Install SIOS LifeKeeper on the secondary virtual machine and join it to the same SIOS LifeKeeper cluster as the primary virtual machine.
  7. Extend the SIOS LifeKeeper SAP HANA resource hierarchy from the primary virtual machine to the secondary virtual machine and configure the replication mode and the failover policies.
  8. Configure the load balancer to direct traffic to the SAP HANA systems and the SIOS LifeKeeper nodes.
  9. Test the high availability and disaster recovery scenarios by simulating failures and verifying the failover and failback operations.

 

Architecture Design

Lets mount and install SIOS

 

Pre-requisite

vmslvascs1:~ # suseconnect -p sle-module-legacy/15.5/x86_64

Registering system to registration proxy https://smt-azure.susecloud.net

Updating system details on https://smt-azure.susecloud.net ...

Activating sle-module-legacy 15.5 x86_64 ...

-> Adding service to system ...

-> Installing release package ...

Successfully registered system

entry.title: INTRODUCTION TO GROUP REPORTING IN SAP S/4HANA (PART I)
entry.content:
1. WHAT IS GROUP REPORTING?

SAP Group Reporting is the new consolidation solution for SAP on the S/4HANA platform delivered on 1809 release. It provides functions for group accounting and management reporting by supporting process control, data collection, data quality control, consolidation, and reporting.

SAP positions Group Reporting as their new, strategic solution for all customers who want a future-oriented, complete solution for consolidation. Much is invested until SAP S/4HANA 2023 release in giving this new solution all the functionality, power and experience of all other SAP consolidation solutions. It is available as a cloud and in on-premise version, and it runs directly on the financial data in the S/4HANA system.

2. WHAT ARE THE MAIN FEATURES?

The new Group Reporting tool provides a smooth process between the local and group closing activities since both are in the same database within SAP S/4HANA. It is well-integrated with SAP Analytics Cloud and offers the following capabilities:

  • Direct access to legal and management accounting from the universal journal, without needing of an ETL or data warehousing tool to transfer the financial records from the transaction system to the consolidation system.
  • Customers can perform consolidation and planning activities in a single application and in real time, having a detailed perspective of the business at any point in the period. That speeds up the closing process to provide more time to analyze and take business decisions upon the results.
  • Introduces Financial Statement (FS) Items, replacing the Group Chart of Accounts. The set up and maintenance of FS Items is managed inside Group Reporting, which means Operating Chart of Accounts is not affected.
  • Rule based eliminations, validations and reports by desired breakdown items are shared for both local and group closing activities.
  • Fully enabled drill-down reporting to the line-items of the entity, leveraging all details of the underlying Universal Journal, having a significant granularity of data and a clear roadmap for integration with SAP Disclosure Management.

3. SYSTEM INTEGRATION

If part of the organization is not running within the S/4HANA Finance system, the data can be loaded via the SAP Group Reporting Data Collection module. It is an additional Cloud-based Data Collection application delivered on the SAP Cloud Platform, with native integration to S/4HANA Group Reporting. It helps to access data that is not in S/4HANA and for data Transformation.

If you want to visualize and present the figures, or add advanced planning & forecasting, the system connects directly to the SAP Analytics Cloud. This Cloud solution  provides deep insights into the business using reporting and data visualization, which will help making better decisions. With live data connections, the integration between SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting and SAP Analytics Cloud enables business analysis at group or corporate level without data replication.

There is a roadmap to connect SAP group reporting to SAP Disclosure Management to help you deliver the final stage reporting. SAP Disclosure Management is designed to produce, fill and publish your financial statements and reports, with embedded workflow, collaborative and audit features.

 

4. BASIC FLOW

SAP Group Reporting obtains the financial records directly from the SAP S/4HANA system, feasibly supplemented with external data via API or manual data entry. After this, the data is validated in accordance with the defined requirements. Followed by a currency conversion, manual journal entries and consolidation to the group structure. This consolidation can take place as a matrix, for example by entity, but can also consolidate cost centers over the same data; including intercompany and equity eliminations.

In any time of that process, it is possible to generate reporting at consolidation unit or group level using SAP Group Reporting standard reports, SAP Analytics Cloud, Disclosure Management, Embedded Analytics or Analysis for Office. A reporting solution is available for each requirement.

5. SAP GROUP REPORTING VS SAP BPC

Not so long ago, SAP released the latest version of SAP BPC: SAP BPC 11.1 for BW4/HANA. With SAP Group Reporting another new SAP solution for consolidation is available for the customers. Then, many of them can consider which solution would fit best for their organizations. Although the answer is not always straightforward, the following recommendation can be taken into consideration for most organizations:

  • If the organization has already implemented SAP S/4HANA Finance, and that is also the most important source for the financial records: SAP Group Reporting, often combined with SAP Analytics Cloud is a good option.
  • If there are large number of source systems and it is desired for an on-premise solution for consolidation and planning/budgeting: SAP BPC 11 would be a good solution.

entry.title: Block leave counter solution in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Welcome viewers, it's glad to see you again.

This is one of the most complex scenarios and I tackled with my own solution. 

In this blog I am discussing the configurations of block leave counter which is developed in the demo system.

What is Block leave?
          When an employee applies an annual leave for 10 days or more than that consequently, it is considered as Block leave. Days differs according to your customer requirement.

Yes, I know you were searching for this blog as this is one of the important requirements where customer may request as an essential requirement. I will dive into the configuration part.

Step 1: Creating a custom fields in the Employee Time object definition

Create custom fields as per your requirement in the configure object definition Employee Time
object. Below image is for your reference.

This field was designed to allow employees and HR to indicate whether an employee’s leave request should be blocked under specific conditions. If this field need to display only selecting annual leave. You can configure as below using field value condition and time type code in your custom field.

 

Step 2: Creating a Rule (Block Leave Counter) in Time Type

I have created 3 rules for my scenario and assigned in the time type, start date and end date field. And additionally, 2 rules assigned in the manage data --> time type --> take rules.

  1. Create Block Leave Counter Rule:
    • Create a new rule called Block Leave Counter. This rule will check multiple conditions to block the leave request.
  2. Navigate to Manage Data:
    • Go to Admin Center and access Manage Data.
  3. Select Time Type:
    • Search for the relevant Time Type that is used for leave requests.
  4. Go to Take Rule Section:

Within the selected Time Type, locate the Take Rule section where rules for leave management are configured.

Rule 1. Take Rules under ---> Manage Data (Time Type)

Rule 2. Take Rules under ---> Manage Data (Time type)

Below rules were assigned in the time type, start date, end date fields in the configure object definition.
Rule 3.

Rule 4.

Rule 5 Set Rule on Save:

  • In the EmployeeTime object, configure the rule to trigger on Save. This rule will be executed when a record in the EmployeeTime object is saved. Even if an employee cancelling the leave, block leave will set as 0. where this will avoid the use of integration center to update the leave balance.

Test Output Cases are below.

Before submitting screenshot is below. On selecting 10 days, block leave field will be shown for employee alone as Yes. Another field Block leave status will be marked as yes only after the approval where HR confirm this. 


Once leave approved, output will be as below.

On cancelling leave, block leave will be reset as per rule.



On next time, if employee try to apply leave in same year, block leave status will be yes, as he already took the block leave in this current year. Else the block leave status will be no as default on initiating the leave.

Thanks for referring to my blog. Will reach you in next blog with a more interesting scenario.

Regards,
Aravind R
HXM consultant.

entry.title: Using ERS for Carrier Settlement in SAP Transportation Management (TM)
entry.content:
The ERS (Evaluated Receipt Settlement) procedure in SAP is not new and actually belongs to the Material Management module (MM), but with the embedded SAP TM solution, a few things might be tricky when it comes to setting up the process.

Process Overview

Let’s start with an overall process overview of the settlement process in SAP TM:

  • A Freight Order (FO) is the object in SAP which is used to transport goods from A to B.
  • Based on the freight order, a Freight Settlement Document (FSD) is created which carries the agreed freight charges. The calculation can happen automatically based on Freight Agreements (FA) or manually based on spot rates.
  • When the FSD is posted, SAP creates a Purchase Order (PO) and posts a Service Entry Sheet (SES) for the costs to be paid to the carrier. This PO is not visible to the carrier. Only the FO was communicated to them. So the PO has a technical function, to build accruals for the charges to be paid and to post the incoming invoice from the vendor against it.
  • The final step is to receive the vendor invoice, but with ERS procedure we create the invoice by ourself and submit the invoice proactively to our carrier.
  • The ERS procedure requires to automatically determine the tax code in the Service Purchase Order.

🛠️ Following areas need to be customized so that the ERS process runs smoothly:

1.) Activate tax at charge type

SPRO: Transportation Management –> Settlement –> Freight Settlement –>Integration for Settlement Posting –>Tax

2.) Setup automatic tax code determination

The official help documentation regarding tax code determination can be found here: SAP TM Tax Determination but it’s not well explained from my point of view.

💡 Depending on the transportation route and the executing carrier, the transport costs must be taxed as domestic service, as reverse charge or w/o taxes for export business.

We need following criteria for the tax code determination

  • Country of the service buying plant
  • Country of the executing carrier
  • Freight order departure country
  • Freight order destination country
  • Tax indicator (in case there are exceptions)

The SPRO items:

Now the steps in detail explained:

a.) Check if all required fields are available in the field catalog for condition tables.

In my case (version 2023) I needed to add the following two fields.

b.) Create a new condition table

⚠️Pay attention on the destination country field. In the SAP documentation, it is LAND1 but you must choose LLAND.

The technical view of the condition table should look like this

c.) Create an access sequence

Choose the created condition table. Most probably, you’ll see an error for LLAND. Select the field and click on “Field Catalog”. There you choose KOMK-LAND1. This is explained in SAP note 3121236

Now, the access sequence should have no errors.

d.) Create a condition type for the tax

Maintain the data in Control Data 1 like on the screenshot and assign your new access sequence.

 

e.) Create the required condition records in transaction MEK1

In this example, our buying plant is in Germany and our carrier based in Germany as well. If the transport service is a domestic transport, then tax code V1 (full input tax) shall be assigned. If we export to Serbia (3rd country from EU perspective), then we assign tax code V0 (no tax). If the carrier is not a German company but based within the European Union, then you might need to add a tax code for “Reverse Charge”.

f.) Create or update PO pricing schema

In my case, I created a dedicated pricing schema for carriers which is determined with a schema group assigned to the relevant vendors (carriers). Of course, you can add the new condition type to an existing pricing schema. You just need to assure that there is no conflict with an existing tax determination logic.

Pricing schema with new condition type

Schema group (Z1)

Schema determination

Assignment of the schema group in the vendor business partner

 

3.) Setup ERS invoice output

Go to transaction NACE, application MR and create a condition record for the output type ERS. If standard customizing is missing, copy it from client 000.

In this case, I want a print out, but in practice you might send the credit note via E-Mail.

4.) Activate ERS for the carrier in the BP master data

In the Vendor / Supplier role of your carrier, navigate to the desired purchasing organization, and activate the checkboxes for “GR-Based Inv. Verif” & “AutoGRSetmt.Ret”.

That should be all.

 

So now a quick process walk-through.

Freight Settlement Document

PO and Service Entry Sheet showing the correct tax code

 

Transaction MRRL to trigger the creation of the credit note

Transaction MR90 to trigger the output of the created credit note

Finally, the FSD is updated with the credit note and has status “Invoice Verified”.

 

💡The whole process with all the transactions / apps involved can be fully automated.

  • Schedule report: /SCMTMS/SFIR_TRANSFER_BATCH to create FSD.
  • Schedule SM37 jobs for MRRL and MR90.

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, creating credit notes for carriers is a more efficient approach than waiting for potentially incorrect invoices. This method not only speeds up the settlement process but also enhances accuracy and control over financial transactions. By automating this procedure, businesses can reduce administrative overhead, improve cash flow management, and ensure greater transparency in their operations. This shift towards credit notes ultimately leads to a more streamlined and reliable carrier settlement process.

References

  • SAP Help Portal Tax Determination | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Community, @AtulGokhale  SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Transportation Management… – SAP Community
  • SAP Note 3121236 for condition table issues for field LLAND 3121236 – Zugriffsfolgen-Konditionstabellenfeldzuordnungsproblem – SAP for Me
  • SAP Note 2424664 for tax code determination, master data setup 2424664 – Stammdateneinrichtung für Steuerkennzeichen in SAP TM und ERP – SAP for Me

SAP Transportation Management

entry.title: 1/3 From JavaScript to TypeScript: How We Enhanced Code Reliability in SAPUI5 Development
entry.content:
In our development team, we’ve always emphasized the importance of quality code. On top of automated code checks we implemented the four-eye principle for pull request approval, ensuring that every piece of code was reviewed by at least two developers before being merged. Yet, despite these precautions, certain issues with JavaScript continued to slip through the cracks and make their way into production.

 

The Challenges We Faced with JavaScript

While JavaScript is an incredibly versatile language, its flexibility came with some serious challenges. Even with thorough reviews, it was nearly impossible to ensure that:

  • All conditions were correct: Reviewers could miss subtle logical flaws in complex conditionals.
  • Edge cases were handled properly: Unhandled edge cases often led to unexpected behavior in production, which only became apparent after users encountered them.
  • Accessing undefined properties: JavaScript’s dynamic typing meant that developers sometimes accessed undefined or null properties, leading to runtime errors that weren’t caught until much later.

These challenges caused bugs and errors to creep into customer systems, leading to delays in issue resolution and frustration for both our team and clients. Even with strict code reviews, the lack of static typing in JavaScript made it difficult to guarantee that all possible cases were covered.

Source: https://i.sstatic.net/QmW6V.png

 

Enter TypeScript: A Game Changer for Code Quality

To address these issues, we decided to transition from JavaScript to TypeScript, and the results have been remarkable. TypeScript’s static typing system has completely changed the way we approach development, making our code more reliable, maintainable, and less prone to errors.

With TypeScript, even junior developers are empowered to write higher-quality code. The compiler provides instant feedback on potential issues, such as incorrect conditions or missing edge case handling. This leads to:

  • Immediate detection of issues: Developers are notified of potential problems, such as accessing undefined properties or incorrect data types, during development instead of at runtime.
  • Increased accountability: TypeScript forces developers to handle edge cases explicitly, ensuring that conditions and logic are sound before the code is even reviewed.

Cleaner, more maintainable code: The ability to define types, interfaces, and classes makes the codebase easier to understand and work with, even for team members who weren’t involved in its original development.

Source: https://i.sstatic.net/XXVvo.png

 

The Benefits We've Seen So Far

Since moving to TypeScript, we’ve seen a dramatic reduction in the number of bugs and errors that make it into production. The code we produce is now far more reliable, and issues that would have gone unnoticed in JavaScript are caught early, often before they even leave the developer's machine.

What’s more, junior developers are writing better, more robust code right from the start. The TypeScript compiler acts as a mentor, guiding them through potential issues and ensuring that all logical paths are accounted for.

OpenAI. (2024). A cartoon illustration of a software web developer sitting at a desk, coding on a computer and being controlled by TypeScript compiler [AI-generated image]. DALL-E

 

 

Conclusion: Why TypeScript is Our New Standard

While JavaScript has its place, TypeScript has proven itself as a much-needed improvement for our SAPUI5 applications. With its static typing, we can catch more issues during development, produce cleaner code, and deliver more reliable applications to our customers.

Moving forward, TypeScript will be the standard for all of our new projects, and we’re excited to see how this change continues to improve our development process.

Next time, we will dive deeper into how your team can adopt this technology and how to support developers in reaching the level required to produce SAPUI5 applications written in TypeScript.

entry.title: Re: Automatic selection of required Task list while resul...
entry.content:
Hi Abbas,

To my knowledge this is not possible, as the system only can assign a unique inspection plan. If not a single/unique inspection plan can be determined by the system you should assign it manually to the inspection lot.

Alternatives to make plans unique: adjust the validity dates of the plans, assign specific suppliers to the inspection plans, delete a inspection plan so only one plan exists for the material.

Herman.

entry.title: 360° of SAP Analytics Cloud Performance Optimization
entry.content:
The importance of performance in reporting and analytics cannot be explained away. Efficient performance optimization in reporting ensures timely insights and enhances decision-making processes.  

To adequately address this crucial issue, we want to introduce our brand-new free learning journey, Managing Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud, designed to equip you with valuable skills in optimizing performance within SAP Analytics Cloud. 

This learning journey covers a comprehensive range of topics to elevate your performance optimization skills.  

  1. You will start by understanding the SAP Analytics Cloud architecture and its impact on performance. 
  2. Next, you will dive into performance troubleshooting using real-world scenarios and uncovering individual metrics to analyze performance.  
  3. The learning journey also includes an exploration of key tools used in performance analysis and measurement. 
  4. You will explore the relationship between story design and performance and dig deeper, designing performance optimized data visualization using pages and widgets.  
  5. You will use specific settings and features that will optimize performance during data loading. 
  6. Finally, you will explore scripting performance by analyzing the story script to identify and solve performance issues.  

We invite you to join this advanced learning journey and master the art of performance optimization in SAP Analytics Cloud. 

If you want to complement your performance skill set with an expert-guided hands-on session, explore our German SAP Data & Analytics Forum Agenda in November 2024.    

SAP Product Learning CoE Analytics

entry.title: Use SAP CAP SAPUI5 Smart Filter to Show Value Help that are not Associated Entitites
entry.content:
How to Use SAP CAP and SAPUI5 Smart Filter to Show Value Help for Fields that are not Associated Entities

In cases where you need to allow the user to select values through a Value Help for a specific field that is not related to another table, you can achieve this behavior in the following way:

1. Create a View that Contains the Values for the Value Help

First, you need to create a view that only contains the values you want to display in the Value Help. This view should perform a SELECT on the entity from which you need to retrieve the values and use a GROUP BY to avoid repeating information.

 

@CDS.odata.valuelist
view Modules as
    select from GlobalSetting {
        key GlobalSettingModule
    }
    where
        GlobalSettingModule is not null
    group by
        GlobalSettingModule;

 

2. Add @Common Annotations in the Main View

In the view, entity, or projection that you are using to build the Smart Table, you need to add @Common annotations. These annotations will serve as input for CAP to determine that the previously created view should be used for the Value Help.

 

view ViewGlobalSettingPatterns as
    select from GlobalSetting {
        *,
        @Common.ValueList.CollectionPath: 'Module'
        @Common.ValueList.Parameters    : [{
            $Type            : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
            LocalDataProperty: 'GlobalSettingModule',
            ValueListProperty: 'GlobalSettingModule'
        }, {
            $Type            : 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly',
            ValueListProperty: 'GlobalSettingModule'
        }]
        GlobalSettingModule
    }
    where
        GlobalSettingClassification = 'PATTERN';

 

3. Expose the Views in the Service

You need to expose the new views so that the service can access them and use them as a data source.

 

service MasterDataService {
    entity GlobalSetting             as projection on model.GlobalSetting;
    @readonly
    entity ViewGlobalSettingPatterns as projection on model.ViewGlobalSettingPatterns;
    @readonly
    @CDS.odata.valuelist
    entity Type                      as projection on model.Types;
    @readonly
    entity Module                    as projection on model.Modules;
}

 

4. Verify the OData Metadata

It is important to verify that the metadata correctly shows the annotations. The CollectionPath is the key point since it tells CAP which resource will be used to display the data in the Value Help.

The metadata should look something like this:

 

<Annotations xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Target="MasterDataService.ViewGlobalSettingPatterns/GlobalSettingModule">
    <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
        <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
            <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Module"/>
            <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                <Collection>
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                        <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="GlobalSettingModule"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="GlobalSettingModule"/>
                    </Record>
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="GlobalSettingModule"/>
                    </Record>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
        </Record>
    </Annotation>
</Annotations>

 

5. Configure the Filter in the Smart Component

Finally, you just need to configure the Smart Filter, where you specify which field will display the Value Help.

 

<smartFilterBar:ControlConfiguration
    key="GlobalSettingModule"
    label="Module"
    visibleInAdvancedArea="true"
    hasValueHelpDialog="true"/>

 

 

Result

Anthony Ramos Romero.

entry.title: Re: [FSCM Collections]: Extend Manage Customer Line Items(App F0711) – OData MPC & DPC Code Enha
entry.content:
hi @robinthakral ,
had similar requirements, long ago.

Have some notes, where I obviously extended the CDS view and brute-force-inserted the fields in supporting DDIC-structures to get F0711 working. yes, NOT clean core, just dirty stuff to get customer happy.

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZEIARLINEITEM'
@EndUserText.label: 'Custom fields for F0711'


/* Requirement:
   Additional columns in F0711.

   Problem:
   SAP Key User Extensibility (TCODE SCFD_REGISTRY)
   does not support code-free extension of F0711 yet.
   only in S/4HANA Cloud.

   Remedy:
   extend the view with manual magic. */


extend view I_ARLineItem with ZE_I_ARLineItem
  association [0..1] to bseg as _BSEG on  _BSEG.bukrs = P_ARLineItem_0.companycode
                                      and _BSEG.belnr = P_ARLineItem_0.accountingdocument
                                      and _BSEG.gjahr = P_ARLineItem_0.fiscalyear
                                      and _BSEG.docln = P_ARLineItem_0.accountingdocumentitem


  association [0..1] to bkpf as _BKPF on  _BKPF.bukrs = P_ARLineItem_0.companycode
                                      and _BKPF.belnr = P_ARLineItem_0.accountingdocument
                                      and _BKPF.gjahr = P_ARLineItem_0.fiscalyear

{
  _BSEG.zz1_salesteam_jei,

  _BKPF.bktxt    as zzBKTXT,
  _BKPF.xref1_hd as zzXREF1_HD,
  _BKPF.xref2_hd as zzXREF2_HD

}


my notes contain the name of a structure "FAR_IFIARLINEITEM_EX" as well. But I was lazy with full documentation...

in fact, F0711 is a complete clusterf*ck in terms of "add customer fields". And almost every customer needs this app WITH customer fields. SAP should do better and deliver a modernized variant of this app.

regards,
Christian
 
p.s. you wrote "Fiori App F12". seems to be a typo.

entry.title: Re: Advice on Reducing DHCDC_RTSTORE Table Size in S/4HANA
entry.content:
@Savio_Dmello
Thanks for editing the comment - appreciated.

The data of DHCDC_RTSTORE should always be deleted by the application - also in the case of failures - it is not necessary to manually delete data.
If data gets stuck its either a bug in the application or the replication was paused or disconnected from the source for some reason.
In this case there will be a cleanup performed after the defined retention time.
Hence, if the table is growing it is either a bug (-> support incident), or a misconfiguration of the retention.

entry.title: Neue UCC Angebote zum Thema Process Automation
entry.content:
Liebe Lehrenden in DACH,

es freut mich die nachfolgenden Information zu neuen Lernangeboten der UCCs mit Ihnen zu teilen.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen

Andre Biener

Director DACH, SAP University Alliances

******************

Neue SAP Process Automation Lernumgebung

Die neu entwickelte SAP Process Automation Lernumgebung verwendet den SAP-Cloud-Service SAP Build Process Automation. Er ist Teil der Low-code/No-code-Lösungsfamilie SAP Build. In Zukunft sollen weitere Komponenten wie SAP Build Apps und SAP Build Code zur Verfügung gestellt werden.

SAP Build Process Automation dient der Teil- oder Vollautomatisierung einfacher, meist repetitiver menschlicher Aufgaben. Intelligente Prozessautomatisierung soll dabei die Effizienz einfacher Betriebsprozesse verbessern, den Durchsatz und die Durchlaufgeschwindigkeit erhöhen sowie menschliche Fehler vermeiden. Dabei können in SAP Build Process Automation Prozesse oder Automationen modelliert, ausgeführt und ausgewertet werden.

Das entwickelte Curriculum verwendet das bekannte Modellunternehmen Global Bike und besteht aus einer Einführung mittels Folien, einer Reihe von einfachen Übungen und einer Fallstudie. In den Übungen werden die Lernenden in die grundlegenden Funktionen der Prozessautomatisierung mit SAP Build Process Automation eingeführt. Sie lernen zu Beginn Anwendungen und Automationen am Beispiel eines einfachen Hello-World-Beispiels kennen. 

In der Fallstudie durchlaufen die Lernenden ein komplexes Projekt, welches ihnen zeigt, wie Global Bike die geplante Verfügbarkeit von Leihfahrrädern an den Standorten New York City und München basierend auf der Wettervorhersage für die kommenden Tage plant. Dazu liest die zu entwickelnde Automation Temperaturen, Tagesdaten und Orte von der Google-Wetterseite und verarbeitet diese weiter. Studierende lernen, wie einmal erstellte Automationen wiederholt und fehlerfrei die Daten beschaffen, aufbereiten und zu Unternehmensentscheidungen heranziehen.

Wir laden Sie herzlich ein, dieses spannende Curriculum näher kennenzulernen und gemeinsam die Möglichkeiten von SAP BTP Process Automation zu entdecken.

Melden Sie sich jetzt für unsere kostenfreie SAP BTP Schulung an:

  • 8. November: SAP BTP Process Automation

Bei Interesse wenden Sie sich bitte an customer-relations@ucc.ovgu.de.

Viele Grüße das Team vom SAP UCC Magdeburg

entry.title: Grant users read-only access to the context of a sap build process automation workflow
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently facing the issue, that I want to grant read-only access to the context of a sap process automation (SBPA) workflow to my end users, via a REST call. Hereby, I find myself in the following scenario. I created a custom UI5-Application that uses the instance-id of a workflow to display the context. Of course, I want to design the authorization in such a way that the user is not able to manipulate the call to the SBPA API by,  for example, sending a post request via Postman.

To implement this, I have evaluated the following options, which have not led to a satisfactory result:

  • Create a destination with client id and client secret of the SBPA service intance
    • By doing this, I enable the user to access the context but also to take the called URL and send a post request, even though the actual credentials are concealed in the destination. 
  • Use API Key's within SBPA
    • I was not able to get them to grant access at all. In addition, I am unsettled by the following statement in this documentation on API keys: "You only need to use API keys for triggers that run automations."

Maybe I am missing something or one of you has another idea and can guide me into the right direction.

Thanks in advance,
Lukas

entry.title: Neue Fallstudie zum Thema Nachhaltigkeit des SAP UCC Magdeburg
entry.content:
Liebe Lehrende in DACH,

ich freue mich Ihnen eine neue Fallstudie zum Thema Nachhaltigkeit des SAP UCC Magdeburg näher vorzustellen.

Bei Interesse wenden Sie sich bitte an die bekannten Kontakte.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen
Andre Biener

Director DACH, SAP University Alliances

**********

"Sustainability in SAP Analytics Cloud"– neue Fallstudie im SAP UCC

Wir freuen uns, Ihnen unsere neue Fallstudie in der SAP Analytics Cloud zum Thema Nachhaltigkeit vorzustellen.

In einer Zeit, in der Klimawandel und Umweltschutz zu zentralen Themen unserer Gesellschaft geworden sind, gewinnt das Thema Nachhaltigkeit immer mehr an Bedeutung. Unternehmen stehen zunehmend vor der Herausforderung, ihre Geschäftsprozesse unter ökologischen Gesichtspunkten zu optimieren und ihren CO2-Fußabdruck zu reduzieren. Um diesen Anforderungen gerecht zu werden, ist eine präzise Datenanalyse unerlässlich.

Das SAP UCC Magdeburg freut sich eine neue Fallstudie aus dem Bereich SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) vorstellen zu können, die sich intensiv mit dem Thema Nachhaltigkeit beschäftigt. Diese Fallstudie bietet ein tiefes Verständnis für die Verknüpfung und Verwendung von Modellen zur Datenanalyse und -visualisierung in SAC.

Unser Szenario stellt die Modelfirma Global Bike als Vorreiter der Nachhaltigkeit dar. Im Rahmen der Vorstellung eines neuen E-Bikes lädt Global Bike Mitarbeiter, Kunden, Lieferanten und Gäste aus Nordamerika und Europa in seinen Flagship Store nach New York City ein. Die Teilnehmer nutzen dabei unterschiedliche Routen und Transportmittel, um zum Veranstaltungsort zu gelangen.

Getreu seiner Nachhaltigkeitsziele als innovatives Mobilitätsunternehmen möchte Global Bike den partiellen CO2-Fußabdruck dieser Veranstaltung auf Basis der Reisedaten aller Teilnehmer berechnen und veranschaulichen.

Unsere Fallstudie umfasst daher den Erstellungsprozess von zwei Modellen und einer Story. Die Fallstudie führt Schritt für Schritt durch den Prozess der Erstellung, Verknüpfung und Nutzung von zwei Modellen in SAP Analytics Cloud. Das erste Modell enthält die Teilnehmerdaten, einschließlich ihrer Herkunftsorte und der genutzten Transportmittel. Das zweite Modell enthält Emissionsdaten der verschiedenen Transportmittel, z.B. Flugzeug, Bahn, Auto, etc. Beide Modelle werden miteinander verknüpft, um eine direkte Zuordnung der Teilnehmerdaten zu den entsprechenden Emissionswerten zu ermöglichen. Basierend auf den verknüpften Modellen wird eine Story in SAC entwickelt, die die Reisedaten und den daraus resultierenden CO2-Fußabdruck visualisiert und analysiert. In der Story und im Modell werden neue Kennzahlen berechnet, um den CO2-Fußabdruck der einzelnen Reisewege der Teilnehmer detailliert darzustellen.

Nachhaltigkeit ist längst kein Trend mehr, sondern eine Notwendigkeit. Unternehmen, die nachhaltig handeln, übernehmen gesellschaftliche Verantwortung und leisten einen wichtigen Beitrag zum Umweltschutz. Darüber hinaus profitieren sie von einem positiven Unternehmensimage, Kosteneinsparungen durch effizientere Ressourcennutzung und sichern langfristig ihre Wettbewerbsfähigkeit.

Das Bewusstsein für Umweltfreundlichkeit wächst weltweit, und sowohl Verbraucher als auch Geschäftspartner legen zunehmend Wert auf nachhaltige Praktiken. Indem Global Bike den CO2-Fußabdruck seiner Veranstaltungen berechnet und transparent kommuniziert, positioniert sich das Unternehmen als verantwortungsvoller Akteur im Mobilitätssektor und setzt ein starkes Zeichen für den Klimaschutz.

Diese Fallstudie zeigt eindrucksvoll, wie SAP Analytics Cloud genutzt werden kann, um komplexe Nachhaltigkeitsfragen zu adressieren. Durch die Erstellung und Verknüpfung von Modellen sowie die Entwicklung aussagekräftiger Stories erhalten Unternehmen wertvolle Einblicke in ihre Umweltauswirkungen und können gezielte Maßnahmen zur Reduktion ihres CO2-Fußabdrucks ergreifen.

Wir laden Sie herzlich ein, diese spannende Fallstudie näher kennenzulernen und gemeinsam die Möglichkeiten von SAP Analytics Cloud im Bereich Nachhaltigkeit zu entdecken. Bei Interesse wenden Sie sich bitte an customer-relations@ucc.ovgu.de.

Viele Grüße das Team vom SAP UCC Magdeburg

entry.title: SAP Discovery Center Innovations: AI Features, Terraform Automation, and More
entry.content:
Tomorrow is the first day of SAP TechEd 2024! It will kick-off as a virtual event and the anticipation and excitement is running incredibly high as we continue our last-minute preparations and rehearsals. If you haven’t had a chance to register, do so now!

Now on to the exciting stuff!

To further empower developers and technical users with advanced tools and expert guidance when adopting SAP products, I am very excited to introduce the following enhancements and updates to SAP Discovery Center.

Explore SAP Business AI Features

SAP Discovery Center has expanded! In addition to the existing three catalogs – SAP BTP Missions, Reference Architectures, and SAP BTP Services, it now offers an even broader range of content with the new SAP Business AI Feature Catalog. You can now browse the comprehensive list of transformative AI features and discover their practical applications and business value. Look around the catalog and dive into the individual features for more details and resources.

SAP Discovery Center

SAP Business AI Feature Catalog

Create Price and Value Estimations

For more insights into your projected AI investments, make use of the new cost and value-added information included in the AI Estimator. You can now calculate net savings for your AI features using a wizard to input your industry, employees, and revenue. This helps estimate costs, needed AI units, and net savings for your SAP AI projects.

AI Feature Estimator: Edit ParametersPrice & Value Estimate

Leverage Quick Account Setup with Terraform

Quick account setup is now seamlessly integrated into several key use cases. This feature simplifies the setup of business use cases from SAP Discovery Center missions, making the process easier and faster. You can easily find all missions featuring quick account setup on the Discovery Center homepage under the new Automated Account Setup Missions category in the Mission Catalog.

Automated Account Setup Missions

Advanced users can delve into the automation steps via the underlying Terraform configurations available on SAP’s public samples repository on GitHub. This fosters transparency and allows you to copy, adjust, and extend configurations, managing your SAP BTP infrastructure as code.

SAP BTP Mission: Quick Account Setup

Experience the Fresh Design + Discovery Center in a Nutshell

Enjoy the new color scheme and streamlined interface of our landing page. In addition, to the fresh look, we have curated a new ‘Discovery Center in a Nutshell’ infographic. Whether you are new to SAP Discovery Center or access it regularly, this infographic provides a consolidated visual view of the content, assets, and guidance provided. From exploration to evaluation, the website provides heaps of essential information and insights to allow you to make informed decisions. With the valuable implementation guidance, you will feel empowered to adopt our products and solutions with confidence.

Discovery Center in a Nutshell

Customize Your Workspace

After logging on, SAP Discovery Center becomes your space. You will immediately find your most recent activities and favorites. To tailor the homepage simply click, ‘Customize Your Space’ and select the content which is most relevant for you.

Customize Your Workspace

Customize Your Workspace Wizard

SAP BTP Solution Diagrams

Based on the SAP Fiori Horizon design system, a new guideline for SAP BTP Solution Diagrams has been released. The new guidelines support integrations, such as standard SAP BTP stencils, and content for draw.io and Lucidchart. Further integration with SAP LeanIX will enhance the seamless diagram creation and management. The newest set of SAP BTP service icons and examples are available on our central GitHub page.

Tailor Reference Architectures to Your Needs

Discover our latest reference architectures that offer best practices and standardized models for optimizing your SAP environment. These architectures simplify deployment and management and are now easily editable via the recently released download feature. The image is available for draw.io download allowing for immediate edits and changes using the integrated BTP stencils and content.

Download Reference Architecture

Discover Top Use Cases

Explore our latest SAP BTP use cases that address the most critical needs of our customers. These curated use cases demonstrate practical applications and showcase the tangible benefits of SAP solutions in real-world scenarios.

SAP Discovery Center at SAP TechEd

Join our SAP TechEd lecture and jumpstart sessions to see these innovations in action! You can find a summary of the activities and highlights in this blog post: SAP Discovery Center at SAP TechEd 2024: Sessions, Resources, and Highlights.

Thoughts?

We hope you find these innovations impactful and beneficial for your business. If you have any feedback, let me know in the comments or message me directly.

Enjoy the event! 😀

entry.title: Re: Date difference between current date and service cont...
entry.content:
Hi Srinivasa

Try this to get a feel for the numbers:

SELECT DATEDIFF(day,now(), serviceContract.endDate)
FROM ServiceContract serviceContract;

Looking at this in my demo environments data, I think this is what you need:

SELECT serviceContract
FROM ServiceContract serviceContract
WHERE DATEDIFF(day, serviceContract.endDate, now()) = 30;

Notice how swapping the dates changes the result from a negative to a positive number.

Best regards

    Raphael Barabas

entry.title: Re: SAP TRM: Separation of loan principal and interest payments
entry.content:
Because the cash flow 1100 Principal and 1200 Norminal interest are linked to different update types. It's easy for you to post to different GL accounts for principal posing and interest payment posting. I simulate your case in our public cloud internal system. 

Simulation in internal system:

  1. Create an intercompany transaction of 55I;

 

Check cash flows:

 

 

1100 Principal Increase

1200 Norminal interest

1120 Final repayment

Please ignore 1905 and 1906

2. TBB1 do the posting for Principal;

 

GL account for principal: 12021200 Short Term Investments, Affiliated, Consolid.(TRM)

3. TBB1 Post interest

For received cash interest, the GL account is 70200200 Interest Income, Affiliated, Consolidation(TRM)

This way the Principal and interest payment are using different GL accounts.

In public cloud we use different account symbol for Principal(1) and interest income (4.1.1). You may refer below settings and do the adjustment accordingly to meet your specific requirement. 

IMG Settings:             

KONTENFINDUNG Define Account Determination for Treasury and Risk Management

Cash Flow

Update type

Posting spec

Posting spec description

Posting Key(D)

Account Symbol(D)

Posting Key(C)

Account Symbol(C)

1100 Principal Increase

MM1100-

11100

Position to Bank Clearing

40

1

40

3

1200 Norminal interest

MM1200+

13100

Bank Clearing to Interest Revenue

40

3

50

entry.title: Re: Using table alias in order by of window expressions - ABAP open SQL
entry.content:
Thank you for the reply @Marian_Zeis. The above syntax will work perfectly as long as only one table has EBELN field in the Table join.

However, if both Table-A & Table-B has EBELN field, then the system throws the error

'The column name or association "EBELN" is ambiguous, which means it occurs in multiple tables'.

To overcome this, I provide alias name of the table to the field EBELN [Ex: a~ebeln].

Example

FIRST_VALUE( ebeln ) OVER( PARTITION BY a~belnr, a~rbukrs, a~gjahr
ORDER BY a~ebeln DESCENDING ) AS ebeln

Then the system throws the below error

"~" is not allowed here. " ) " is expected.

Any leads will be appreciated. TIA.

 

Regards,

Prabha.

entry.title: Re: Using table alias in order by of window expressions - ABAP open SQL
entry.content:
yeah, its not easy, i cant try now, but do subquery work?

SELECT
    subquery~laufd,
    subquery~laufi,
    subquery~chect,
    subquery~zbukr,
    subquery~pridt,
    subquery~lifnr,
    subquery~kunnr,
    subquery~belnr,
    subquery~bldat,
    subquery~rwbtr,
    subquery~sgtxt,
    subquery~waers,
    subquery~voidd,
    subquery~shkzg,
    subquery~vblnr,
    subquery~zwels,
    subquery~hbkid,
    subquery~hktid,
    subquery~rzawe,
    subquery~text2,
    subquery~xblnr,
    subquery~zfbdt,
    subquery~zbd1t,
    subquery~zbd2t,
    subquery~zbd3t,
    subquery~dmbtr,
    subquery~zpstl,
    subquery~zort1,
    subquery~zstra,
    subquery~zpfac,
    subquery~landx,
    subquery~bezei,
    subquery~xbanc,
    subquery~bancd,
    FIRST_VALUE( ebeln ) OVER( PARTITION BY belnr, rbukrs, gjahr
                               ORDER BY ebeln DESCENDING ) AS ebeln
FROM (
    SELECT
        p~laufd,
        p~laufi,
        chect,
        p~zbukr,
        pridt,
        p~lifnr,
        p~kunnr,
        rp~belnr,
        rp~bldat,
        p~rwbtr,
        b~sgtxt,
        rp~waers,
        p~voidd,
        rp~shkzg,
        p~vblnr,
        zwels,
        p~hbkid,
        p~hktid,
        p~rzawe,
        text2,
        xblnr,
        rp~zfbdt,
        rp~zbd1t,
        rp~zbd2t,
        rp~zbd3t,
        rp~dmbtr,
        zpstl,
        zort1,
        zstra,
        zpfac,
        landx,
        bezei,
        xbanc,
        bancd,
        a~ebeln
    FROM payr AS p
    INNER JOIN regup AS rp
      ON  p~laufd = rp~laufd
      AND p~laufi = rp~laufi
      AND p~lifnr = rp~lifnr
      AND p~kunnr = rp~kunnr
      AND p~vblnr = rp~vblnr
    INNER JOIN bseg AS b
      ON  rp~belnr = b~belnr
      AND rp~bukrs = b~bukrs
      AND rp~gjahr = b~gjahr
      AND rp~buzei = b~buzei
    LEFT OUTER JOIN acdoca AS a
      ON  rp~belnr = a~belnr
      AND rp~bukrs = a~rbukrs
      AND rp~gjahr = a~gjahr
      AND a~rldnr = @LC_01
      AND a~blart = @LC_re
      AND a~ebeln <> @space
) AS subquery
INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_check).

entry.title: Quick hacks: Displaying fields
entry.content:
Like most developers, I'm very lazy and want to do things the neat, quick and cool way whenever I can. Today's quick hack is showing you how I very quickly display the fields of a data resource, whether it be on a list or on a detail page.

This hack is specifically for those text or number type fields that you don't want to style in any specific way, but want to just show with a label and the content below.

Example of fields with label and value

Whenever I need to do this, I take the Object I want to display and do a nifty formula using MAP, KEYS and LOOKUP. The following example is done in a situation like above when the Object I want to display is repeated.

 

MAP(KEYS(repeated.current), {key: item, value: LOOKUP(repeated.current, item)})

 

If you don't want all of the fields displayed (or want to display some fields in a different way), you can omit those fields using OMIT_KEYS formula, e.g.

MAP(KEYS(OMIT_KEYS(repeated.current, ["id", "image"])), {key: item, value: LOOKUP(repeated.current, item)})

In addition, you could of course for example style the field names (e.g. capitalizing with CAPITALIZE).

Hope this helps or gives you ideas for how to use formulas in your apps!

entry.title: Re: PaPM function doesn't get deployed at first, and only...
entry.content:
Hello Leonardo,

I understand that you are encountering this issue when executing PaPM transactions. To fix the issue, kindly follow the steps below:

1. Transaction SICF: Please make sure that node WDR_APC is activated
2. Web Dynpro application WD_GLOBAL_SETTING: Please make sure that the parameter WDNOTIFICATIONSERVICE is set to value PUSH. You can use this parameter also as URL Parameter.
3. SAP Note 2748620 - Requirements for Notification Service through WebSocket communication protocol

If still does not work, incident can be created and assign to Webdynpro component BC-WD-ABA-RUN.

Hope this helps!

Best regards,
Kevin

entry.title: Re: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in...
entry.content: Hi Dilip, the internal behaviour with dump marker should be similar for MSA/WSB if I'm right. Regarding the data in queue, you can check this: "For each DSI that resumes with the skip to resync marker parameter, the DSI outbound queue records in the Replication Server system log that DSI has received the resync marker and also records that from that point forward, DSI rejects committed transactions until it receives the dump database marker." https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_REPLICATION_SERVER/62f3e3a6d49342cf9b7d72ee726a48b0/fd0bedadbd1c1014b1beabd7f2f84de7.html?locale=en-US&q=resync+marker

entry.title: Re: FocusBuild - TOCs not imported immediately
entry.content: Hi Shawn, thanks for your help. I took a look at transaction type SM1J in SM34->AIC_SETTINGS2 (there are no ZXXX transaction types in our systems). Under E004, the action is COPY_ALL_ENH. So TOC should be imported automatically. I will open a support ticket with SAP after getting some more information about the RFCs which show this behaviour.

entry.title: Re: FocusBuild - TOCs not imported immediately
